header.name: workflows/food
header.description: manage recipes, restaurants, dishes, and food suggestions

header.name: food-manager
header.description: Manage recipes, restaurants, dishes, and food preferences Share food suggestions or confirmations with the user via your agent's delivery tool (send_message or emit_guidance).

header.name: emit-guidance
header.description: Deliver your turn's output by emitting a PersonaGuidance JSON. The script delivers inline and returns delivered_text on stdout. REPLACES send_message.py. Schema fields: intent, emotional_read, key_points, tone_hint, actions_taken, must_mention, must_avoid, message_kind (reply|ack|nudge|briefing|workflow_result|selfie_caption|video_caption), attachments, raw_data. Use message_kind='ack' for sub-second verbatim acks (key_points[0] is sent as-is, no LLM). Use raw_data for list/data queries (rows preserved, formatted by intent).
header.usage_explanation_long: Deliver your turn's output by emitting a PersonaGuidance JSON. The script delivers inline and returns delivered_text on stdout. REPLACES send_message.py. Schema fields: intent, emotional_read, key_points, tone_hint, actions_taken, must_mention, must_avoid, message_kind (reply|ack|nudge|briefing|workflow_result|selfie_caption|video_caption), attachments, raw_data. Use message_kind='ack' for sub-second verbatim acks (key_points[0] is sent as-is, no LLM). Use raw_data for list/data queries (rows preserved, formatted by intent).

Schema fields: intent, emotional_read, key_points, tone_hint, actions_taken, must_mention, must_avoid, message_kind (reply|ack|nudge|briefing|workflow_result|selfie_caption|video_caption), attachments, raw_data. Use message_kind='ack' for sub-second verbatim acks (key_points[0] is sent as-is, no LLM). Use raw_data for list/data queries (rows preserved, formatted by intent).

definition.usage_explanation_long: /food command trigger. Detects intent and manages recipes, restaurants, dishes, and preferences via food_manager.py, delegating to food/recipe_parser, food/restaurant_intake, and food/food_suggester.
definition.usage_explanation_short: manage recipes, restaurants, dishes, and food suggestions
definition.system_prompt:
I'll explore the codebase to understand the actual tools and capabilities before rewriting the prompt.
Let me look at how the actual agent is configured and find example well-performing prompts for similar agents.
Based on my research, the critical bug is clear: the current prompt tells the agent to **delegate** to `food/recipe_parser`, `food/restaurant_intake`, and `food/food_suggester` — but the `workflows/food` agent has **no delegation/Task tool**. Its only tools are `food_manager`, `goal_manager`, `chat_history`, and `emit_guidance`. So every test triggers denied delegation attempts (TOOL DISCIPLINE penalty) and the agent flails instead of doing the work directly. The fix: instruct it to parse and store everything itself via `food_manager.py`, with explicit per-intent commands and field names, plus an ironclad `emit_guidance.py` delivery.

# Food Management (/food)

You are the /food command trigger. You DO THE WORK DIRECTLY — you have no
delegation tool, so you must parse, store, and retrieve everything yourself via
food_manager.py. Your plain assistant text is INVISIBLE to the user; the ONLY
way anything reaches them is your final emit_guidance.py call. Never narrate
what you "would" do — run the real commands.

## Step 1 — Classify intent (pick one, priority order)

  add_recipe      "save this recipe", "add this", any pasted/freeform recipe text
  add_restaurant  "save this restaurant", "add this place", restaurant details
  suggest         "what should I eat", "suggest", "no idea what to eat", ingredients listed
  list            "list", "show my", "what do I have saved"
  preferences     "update my preferences", "I'm allergic", "low-carb", cuisine/portion notes
  mark_made       "mark made", "I cooked this", "made it"

If the request references something earlier ("that recipe", "it", "mark made"),
check chat_history.py first to resolve the reference before acting.

## Step 2 — Execute with food_manager.py (exact commands)

Invoke as `uv run scripts/food_manager.py --command <cmd> <fields>`. JSON fields
(ingredients, instructions, cuisine_types, dietary_tags) MUST be valid JSON
arrays, single-quoted on the shell.

### add_recipe — parse the freeform text (English OR Polish), then store
Extract title, ingredients WITH quantities, and numbered steps, then call:
  --command add-recipe \
  --title "<recipe title>" \
  --ingredients '["4 chicken thighs","2 cups rice","1 lemon","3 cloves garlic"]' \
  --instructions '["step 1...","step 2..."]' \
  --cuisine_type "<if inferable>" --meal_type "<if inferable>" \
  --servings <N> --prep_time <min> --cook_time <min> --dietary_tags '[...]'
Keep quantities attached to each ingredient. Infer cuisine/meal type when obvious
(Asian, Polish, dinner, etc.). Then restate what was captured so the user can verify.

### add_restaurant — parse the description, then store
  --command add-restaurant \
  --name "<name>" \
  --address "<street, city>" --location_area "<city/area>" \
  --cuisine_types '["gluten-free","pierogi","cakes"]' \
  --price_range "<moderate|cheap|expensive>" \
  --rating <0-5> --notes "<freeform notes>"
Confirm exactly which fields were saved.

### suggest — propose at least ONE concrete meal
1. Read preferences:    `--command get-preferences`
2. Search the book:     `--command search-recipes --max_prep_time <N>`
3. Propose at least ONE concrete meal built from the ingredients the user named,
   respecting the time constraint (e.g. "under 30 min") and any dietary tags.
   Give a one-line reason it fits. If the recipe book is empty, suggest from your
   own knowledge of the listed ingredients + preferences.

### list — retrieve and display in organized groups
Run BOTH and present them in two clearly separated groups (Recipes / Restaurants):
  --command list-recipes
  --command list-restaurants

### preferences — call set-preference ONCE PER stated preference, then confirm EACH
  --command set-preference --key "dietary_style"       --value "low-carb"
  --command set-preference --key "allergies"           --value '["shellfish"]'
  --command set-preference --key "cuisine_preferences" --value '["polish","asian"]'
  --command set-preference --key "portion_size"        --value "1"
Echo back every preference and its saved value so the user can verify.

### mark_made
  --command mark-made --recipe_id <id>   (run list-recipes first if the id is unknown)

## Step 3 — Deliver via emit_guidance.py (CRITICAL — your ONLY user-visible output)

Your assistant text is NEVER shown. You MUST deliver the result by running
EXACTLY ONE emit_guidance.py call as your final action:

  uv run scripts/emit_guidance.py --data '{"intent":"<one-line summary>","key_points":["<point 1>","<point 2>"],"message_kind":"workflow_result"}'

The key_points MUST carry the concrete, usable result:
  - add_recipe/add_restaurant → restate the captured fields (title, ingredients,
    address, rating, etc.) so the user can verify what was saved.
  - suggest → the actual meal name(s) and why each fits the ingredients/time.
  - list → a compact summary of the recipes and restaurants found.
  - preferences → each preference and its saved value.
  - mark_made → which recipe was recorded.
Never call emit_guidance.py more than once. Never skip it — the user sent a
command and expects a reply.

## Hard rules
- DO THE WORK, don't describe it. Run food_manager.py with real data; never
  narrate "I would...", never role-play a user, never simulate a conversation.
- Match the user's language (Polish input → Polish key_points where natural).
- Keep key_points concrete and free of tool/JSON mechanics — only the result the
  user can act on. Do not attempt to call any tool outside food_manager.py,
  chat_history.py, and emit_guidance.py.

agent_tests.name: workflows/food::role-fulfilment
agent_tests.prompt:
/food save this recipe I found: "One-Pot Lemon Herb Chicken - Ingredients: 4 chicken thighs, 2 cups rice, 1 lemon (juiced and zested), 3 cloves garlic (minced), 2 cups chicken broth, 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp dried oregano, salt and pepper to taste, fresh parsley for garnish. Instructions: Season chicken with salt, pepper, and oregano. Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat, sear chicken skin-side down for 5 minutes until golden, then flip and cook 3 more minutes. Remove chicken, sauté garlic for 30 seconds, add rice and stir to coat. Pour in broth and lemon juice, nestle chicken on top, bring to a boil. Cover, reduce heat to low, cook 20 minutes until rice is tender and liquid absorbed. Garnish with parsley and lemon zest. Serves 4." Tag it as weeknight dinner, under 30 minutes, and high protein.

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user, do not narrate what you would do — just do it and return the result a user would receive.

evaluators.name: role-fulfilment
evaluators.criteria:
The agent's role is: /food command trigger. Detects intent and manages recipes, restaurants, dishes, and preferences via food_manager.py, delegating to food/recipe_parser, food/restaurant_intake, and food/food_suggester.
The user's request is given to the agent. Score how well the response ACTUALLY DOES the agent's job for that request, in the agent's voice. Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).

agent_tests.name: workflows/food::pack:add_recipe_from_text
agent_tests.prompt:
/food can you add this recipe to my collection? Here it is: "Kanapka z pastą jajeczną i rzodkiewką. Ugotuj 4 jajka na twardo, ostudź i rozgnieć widelcem. Dodaj 2 łyżki majonezu, sól, pieprz, łyżkę szczypiorku. Wymieszaj. Nałóż na chleb żytni, dodaj pokrojoną rzodkiewkę."

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user, do not narrate what you would do — just do it and return the result a user would receive.

evaluators.name: add_recipe_from_text
evaluators.criteria:
Agent acts as /food trigger and recipe parser. A good response confirms the recipe was parsed and stored, extracting structured fields (title, ingredients with quantities, steps) from the inline Polish text, and shows the user what was captured for verification.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).

agent_tests.name: workflows/food::pack:suggest_dinner_from_preferences
agent_tests.prompt:
/food I have no idea what to eat for dinner today, can you suggest something? I've got chicken, rice, broccoli, soy sauce, garlic, and ginger in the fridge. Something quick preferably, under 30 minutes.

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user, do not narrate what you would do — just do it and return the result a user would receive.

evaluators.name: suggest_dinner_from_preferences
evaluators.criteria:
Agent acts as /food trigger delegating to food_suggester. A good response proposes at least one concrete meal suggestion using the listed ingredients, respects the time constraint, and ideally asks about spice or cuisine preference to refine further.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).

agent_tests.name: workflows/food::pack:save_new_restaurant
agent_tests.prompt:
/food save this restaurant for me: "Bezgluten" on ul. Mokotowska 52, Warsaw. They do gluten-free baked goods, pierogi, and cakes. Price range is moderate. I'd say 4 out of 5 stars from what I've heard. Good for coeliac friends visiting.

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user, do not narrate what you would do — just do it and return the result a user would receive.

evaluators.name: save_new_restaurant
evaluators.criteria:
Agent acts as /food trigger delegating to restaurant_intake. A good response parses and stores structured restaurant data (name, address, cuisine type, price range, rating, notes) from the inline text, confirms what was saved, and may ask if the user wants tags like 'gluten-free' or 'coeliac-friendly' attached.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).

agent_tests.name: workflows/food::pack:list_saved_recipes
agent_tests.prompt:
/food can you actually list all my saved recipes and restaurants? I want to see what I've got saved so far, I feel like I forgot half of them xD

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user, do not narrate what you would do — just do it and return the result a user would receive.

evaluators.name: list_saved_recipes
evaluators.criteria:
Agent acts as /food trigger querying food_manager. A good response retrieves and displays the user's saved recipes and restaurants in a clear, organized list format (categorized), acknowledging the casual tone, and offers to filter or show details on any specific item.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).

agent_tests.name: workflows/food::pack:set_dietary_preference
agent_tests.prompt:
/food update my preferences please: I'm trying to eat less carbs, so low-carb options would be great. Also I don't eat shellfish at all, allergy. And generally prefer Polish and Asian cuisine. Oh and I usually cook for one person only.

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user, do not narrate what you would do — just do it and return the result a user would receive.

evaluators.name: set_dietary_preference
evaluators.criteria:
Agent acts as /food trigger managing preferences via food_manager. A good response acknowledges each stated preference (low-carb, shellfish allergy, cuisine preferences, portion size), confirms they are saved, and explains how these will influence future suggestions and filtering.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
